result:
1. Spoofable rate-limit key via raw `X-Forwarded-For`
evidence: `rl:${req.headers['x-forwarded-for']||req.ip}` reads untrusted header directly
impact: attacker rotates XFF per request, bypasses per-IP limit (repeat of 2025-09 SEV1 brute-force bypass); violates Section 2 rule 2 and `no-direct-xff-read` lint
fix: key on `trustedClientIp(req)` from `src/edge/util/trustedClientIp.ts` — validates proxy chain against `config/trusted_proxies.json`, else falls back to `req.socket.remoteAddress`
verify: unit test — forged `X-Forwarded-For: 1.2.3.4` from non-trusted-proxy peer must key on `socket.remoteAddress`, not `1.2.3.4`; lint run must flag the current line

2. Missing `EXPIRE` → Redis OOM
evidence: `redis.incr(key)` with no paired `expire`; violates `no-unbounded-redis-key` rule
impact: unbounded keys accumulate, repeat of 2025-11 SEV2 Redis OOM / edge outage
fix: `MULTI` pairing `INCR` + `EXPIRE(key, 90)` per Section 4 template; compare returned count to limit, emit RFC 7807 `429` with `retry_after`
verify: integration test asserts `TTL key` > 0 after first hit; load test drives 10k unique keys and confirms Redis `used_memory` plateaus

3. Missing region prefix in key
evidence: key is `rl:{ip}:...` with no region segment; collides across regions
impact: repeat of 2025-12 SEV2 — one busy user throttled globally instead of per-region
fix: `rl:${region}:${trustedClientIp}:${routePrefix}:${minuteBucket}` (region from env / config)
verify: regression test from 2025-12 RCA — same IP keyed in `us-east-1` and `eu-west-1` produces distinct counters

4. Missing route prefix + minute bucket → wrong window / cross-route bleed
evidence: key lacks `{route_prefix}` and `{minute_bucket}` segments mandated in Section 4
impact: all routes share one counter per IP; no sliding window, limits misapplied, per-route config in `config/rate_limits.json` ignored
fix: use documented format `rl:{region}:{ip}:{route_prefix}:{minute_bucket}` with `Math.floor(Date.now()/60000)`
verify: unit test — `GET /a` and `POST /b` from same IP produce independent counters; bucket rolls over at minute boundary

5. No limit comparison / no `429` response
evidence: `incr` result unused; handler proceeds regardless of count
impact: limiter is a no-op — does not throttle; SLO + abuse-protection gap
fix: compare `count > limit` (limit from `config/rate_limits.json`, fallback 120 GET / 30 write), return RFC 7807 problem+json with `type`, `title`, `status: 429`, `detail`, `retry_after: secondsUntilNextBucket()`
verify: test — 121st GET in a minute returns `429` with `application/problem+json` body matching schema

6. No error handling on Redis call → request fails closed/open unpredictably
evidence: `await redis.incr(key)` has no try/catch; transient connection errors propagate as unhandled `500`
impact: Redis blip converts to edge `5xx` spike; violates Section 11 retry guidance (`src/util/retry.ts`, 3× expo backoff)
fix: wrap in retry helper; on final failure, fail-open with a metric `atlas_edge_rate_limit_total{action="errored"}` increment and structured log — do not 500 the user for a limiter outage
verify: inject Redis disconnect in integration test; request still succeeds, `errored` counter increments, no `5xx` emitted
